name: Syn
description: Syn is a Norse goddess associated with doorways, boundaries, and denial. She is one of the ásynjur, the goddesses of Asgard. Her name translates to "denial" or "refusal," reflecting her primary role. Syn guards the doors of halls, particularly those where legal matters are discussed or judgments are made. She is invoked when someone attempts to enter a place or situation unlawfully or unjustly. Her presence ensures fairness and prevents unauthorized access. Syn is also associated with justice and truth, ensuring that only those with legitimate reasons can pass through. She is a powerful force against deceit and wrongdoing, upholding the established order. While not as prominently featured as some other Norse deities, Syn’s importance lies in her unwavering commitment to justice and the preservation of boundaries. She is a guardian against chaos and ensures that the rules are followed. Syn's power is subtle but significant, representing the importance of respecting limits and upholding the law. She is a reminder that actions have consequences and that justice will prevail. Her association with doorways and boundaries extends beyond the physical realm, symbolizing the limits of knowledge, understanding, and personal space. She is a protector of privacy and a symbol of integrity.
subtitle: Goddess of Doorways and Denial

keyMyths.title: Syn's Role in the Halls
keyMyths.description: Syn guards the doors of halls, preventing unjust entry. She ensures only those with legitimate reasons can pass.

keyMyths.title: Syn and Justice
keyMyths.description: Syn is associated with justice and fairness, upholding the law and preventing wrongdoing. She represents the importance of respecting boundaries.

keyMyths.title: Syn's Power of Denial
keyMyths.description: Her name reflects her power to deny access to those who seek to enter unlawfully. She is a force against deceit and chaos.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
